User.title: User
User.description: Represents a registered user of the platform with common identification and role information, applicable to Super Admins, Admins, Drivers, and Passengers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for login and notifications.

name.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the platform (e.g., 'SuperAdmin', 'Admin', 'Driver', 'Passenger').

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user's profile was last updated.

DriverProfile.title: DriverProfile
DriverProfile.description: Contains specific details and operational status for users who act as drivers on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DriverProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity for this driver. (Relationship: User 1:1 DriverProfile)

status.description: The current operational status of the driver (e.g., 'Online', 'Offline', 'OnTrip').

isApproved.description: Indicates if the driver's registration and documents have been approved by an Admin.

rating.description: The average rating received by the driver from passengers.

currentVehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle entity currently assigned to this driver. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:1 Vehicle)

earningsBalance.description: The current earnings balance for the driver.

lastOnlineTime.description: Timestamp when the driver last changed their status to 'Online'.

PassengerProfile.title: PassengerProfile
PassengerProfile.description: Stores specific information for users who use the platform for rides or deliveries as customers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PassengerProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity for this passenger. (Relationship: User 1:1 PassengerProfile)

defaultPickupAddress.description: A default pickup address saved by the passenger.

defaultDropoffAddress.description: A default drop-off address saved by the passenger.

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Details about each vehicle registered in the fleet, including its type, status, and current location.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vehicle entity.

driverId.description: Reference to the DriverProfile entity currently assigned to this vehicle. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:N Vehicle)

type.description: The specific type of the vehicle (e.g., 'Bike', 'Auto', 'Cab', 'Mini Truck', 'Van', 'Pickup Truck', 'Lorry').

category.description: The primary service category this vehicle supports (e.g., 'Passenger', 'Parcel', 'Logistics').

year.description: The manufacturing year of the vehicle.

licensePlate.description: The unique license plate number of the vehicle.

capacityValue.description: The capacity value of the vehicle (e.g., number of seats, weight capacity, volume capacity).

fuelType.description: The type of fuel the vehicle uses (e.g., 'Petrol', 'Diesel', 'Electric').

status.description: The operational status of the vehicle (e.g., 'Active', 'Idle', 'Maintenance', 'Emergency').

lastMaintenanceDate.description: The date of the last recorded maintenance.

nextMaintenanceDate.description: The estimated date for the next maintenance.

currentLatitude.description: The current latitude of the vehicle.

currentLongitude.description: The current longitude of the vehicle.

Ride.title: Ride
Ride.description: Represents a single instance of a service request, which can be a passenger ride, parcel delivery, or logistics transport.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ride entity.

passengerId.description: Reference to the PassengerProfile entity who booked the ride. (Relationship: PassengerProfile 1:N Ride)

driverId.description: Reference to the DriverProfile entity assigned to this ride. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:N Ride)

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle entity used for this ride. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N Ride)

pickupAddress.description: The full address of the pickup location.

pickupLatitude.description: The latitude of the pickup location.

pickupLongitude.description: The longitude of the pickup location.

dropoffAddress.description: The full address of the drop-off location.

dropoffLatitude.description: The latitude of the drop-off location.

dropoffLongitude.description: The longitude of the drop-off location.

estimatedFare.description: The estimated fare for the service at the time of booking.

actualFare.description: The final calculated fare after the service is completed.

distanceKm.description: The total distance covered during the ride in kilometers.

durationMinutes.description: The total duration of the ride in minutes.

status.description: The current status of the ride (e.g., 'Requested', 'Accepted', 'ArrivedAtPickup', 'InProgress', 'Completed', 'Cancelled', 'Declined').

requestTime.description: Timestamp when the ride request was made.

acceptTime.description: Timestamp when a driver accepted the ride request.

arrivalTime.description: Timestamp when the driver arrived at the pickup location.

startTime.description: Timestamp when the ride officially started.

endTime.description: Timestamp when the ride officially ended.

cancellationReason.description: The reason for cancellation if the ride was cancelled.

surgeMultiplier.description: The surge pricing multiplier applied to the fare.

paymentId.description: Reference to the Payment entity for this ride. (Relationship: Payment 1:1 Ride)

driverRatingId.description: Reference to the Rating entity for the driver's rating on this ride. (Relationship: Rating 1:1 Ride)

passengerRatingId.description: Reference to the Rating entity for the passenger's rating on this ride. (Relationship: Rating 1:1 Ride)

routeGeoJson.description: A JSON string representing the GeoJSON LineString of the actual route taken during the ride.

expectedRouteGeoJson.description: A JSON string representing the GeoJSON LineString of the planned route for the ride.

DriverLocationHistory.title: DriverLocationHistory
DriverLocationHistory.description: Records real-time location updates for drivers, primarily used for live tracking and route playback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DriverLocationHistory entity.

driverId.description: Reference to the DriverProfile entity whose location is being recorded. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:N DriverLocationHistory)

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the driver's location.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the driver's location.

speedKmh.description: The speed of the vehicle in kilometers per hour at this timestamp.

headingDegrees.description: The heading or direction of the vehicle in degrees (0-359).

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this location update was recorded.

rideId.description: Optional reference to the Ride entity if the location update is part of an active ride. (Relationship: Ride 1:N DriverLocationHistory)

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records financial transactions related to rides, including amounts, methods, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

rideId.description: Reference to the Ride entity associated with this payment. (Relationship: Ride 1:1 Payment)

payerUserId.description: Reference to the User (Passenger) who made the payment. (Relationship: User 1:N Payment)

payeeUserId.description: Reference to the User (Driver or Platform) who received the payment. (Relationship: User 1:N Payment)

amount.description: The total amount of the payment.

currency.description: The currency in which the payment was made (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

method.description: The payment method used (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'Wallet', 'Cash', 'Bank Transfer').

transactionId.description: An external transaction identifier from the payment gateway.

status.description: The current status of the payment (e.g., 'Pending', 'Completed', 'Failed', 'Refunded').

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the payment transaction occurred.

Rating.title: Rating
Rating.description: Records feedback provided by users for a specific ride or service interaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Rating entity.

rideId.description: Reference to the Ride entity this rating is associated with. (Relationship: Ride 1:N Rating)

raterUserId.description: Reference to the User who provided the rating. (Relationship: User 1:N Rating)

ratedUserId.description: Reference to the User who received the rating. (Relationship: User 1:N Rating)

score.description: The numerical score given in the rating (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: Optional textual comment provided with the rating.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the rating was submitted.

Zone.title: Zone
Zone.description: Defines geographic areas on the map used for geofencing, operational boundaries, or service regions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Zone entity.

name.description: A human-readable name for the zone (e.g., 'Downtown Delivery Area', 'Warehouse A').

type.description: The classification of the zone (e.g., 'Warehouse', 'Delivery Area', 'Restricted Zone', 'Service Area').

description.description: A brief description or purpose of the zone.

polygonCoordinates.description: A JSON string representing an array of coordinate pairs (e.g., '[[lat1, lng1], [lat2, lng2], ...]') defining the polygon boundary of the zone.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Records important system actions and changes for security, compliance, and debugging purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity who performed the action. (Relationship: User 1:N AuditLog)

actionDetails.description: Detailed description of the action, potentially including a JSON string of before/after states or specific parameters.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the action was performed.

ipAddress.description: The IP address from which the action originated.

VehicleDocument.title: VehicleDocument
VehicleDocument.description: Stores details about regulatory or administrative documents associated with a specific vehicle.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VehicleDocument entity.

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle entity this document belongs to. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N VehicleDocument)

documentUrl.description: URL to the digital copy of the document.

issueDate.description: The date when the document was issued.

expiryDate.description: The expiration date of the document.

uploadDate.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded to the system.

DriverDocument.title: DriverDocument
DriverDocument.description: Stores details about regulatory or administrative documents required for a driver.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DriverDocument entity.

driverId.description: Reference to the DriverProfile entity this document belongs to. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:N DriverDocument)

documentType.description: The type of document (e.g., 'License', 'Background Check', 'Medical Certificate').

documentUrl.description: URL to the digital copy of the document.

issueDate.description: The date when the document was issued.

expiryDate.description: The expiration date of the document.

uploadDate.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded to the system.

FuelLog.title: FuelLog
FuelLog.description: Records details of fuel purchases made by drivers for their assigned vehicles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FuelLog entity.

driverId.description: Reference to the DriverProfile entity who recorded the fuel expense. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:N FuelLog)

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle entity for which fuel was purchased. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N FuelLog)

amountLiter.description: The quantity of fuel purchased in liters.

costCurrency.description: The total cost of the fuel purchase.

currency.description: The currency of the fuel purchase cost (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

receiptUrl.description: URL to the digital image or scan of the fuel receipt.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the fuel purchase was made and logged.

definition.description: Stores core user profiles including role (SuperAdmin, Admin, Driver, Passenger). This document is central for Role-Based Access Control (DBAC). Each document's ID matches the Firebase Auth UID. Authorization is primarily based on request.auth.uid matching userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the user, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores specific profiles and operational details for users with the 'Driver' role. Each document's ID matches the corresponding user's Firebase Auth UID. Accessible by the driver themselves, and by Admins/SuperAdmins. DriverId matches the userId in the document for authorization independence.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Documents specific to a driver (e.g., license, background check). Accessible by the driver owner and by Admins/SuperAdmins. Authorization is independent as the driverId is implicit in the path.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver, owner of the document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the driver's document.

definition.description: Records fuel expenses logged by a specific driver. Accessible by the driver owner and by Admins/SuperAdmins. Authorization is independent as the driverId is implicit in the path.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver who logged the fuel expense.

params.name: fuelLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the fuel log entry.

definition.description: Stores specific profiles for users with the 'Passenger' role. Each document's ID matches the corresponding user's Firebase Auth UID. Accessible only by the passenger themselves. PassengerId matches the userId in the document for authorization independence.

params.name: passengerId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the passenger, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores details about each vehicle in the fleet. Managed by Admins/SuperAdmins. Assigned drivers can read their vehicle's details. Includes 'driverId' for the currently assigned driver for authorization.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the vehicle.

definition.description: Stores documents for a specific vehicle. Primarily managed by Admins/SuperAdmins, but assigned drivers can view. Includes denormalized 'assignedDriverId' (from the parent Vehicle) for authorization independence for driver view access.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the vehicle to which the document belongs.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the vehicle's document.

definition.description: Represents a single service request (ride, parcel, logistics). Accessible by the involved passenger, driver, and Admins/SuperAdmins. Includes denormalized 'passengerId' and 'driverId' (which are User.id's) for authorization independence, enabling QAPs for listing rides by user.

params.name: rideId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ride/service request.

definition.description: Records historical location updates for a driver during a specific ride. Accessible by the involved passenger, driver, and Admins/SuperAdmins. Includes denormalized 'ridePassengerId' and 'rideDriverId' (from the parent Ride document) for authorization independence.

params.name: rideId
params.description: The unique identifier of the ride this location history belongs to.

params.name: locationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific location update.

definition.description: Stores the current, real-time location and status of an online driver. This document is frequently updated. Accessible by the driver for writing, and by Admins/SuperAdmins for reading. Passengers on an active ride can also read their assigned driver's live location. Includes denormalized 'currentPassengerId' (if on a ride) for authorization independence for passengers.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver whose live location is recorded.

definition.description: Records financial transactions. Accessible by the payer, payee, and Admins/SuperAdmins. Includes denormalized 'payerUserId' and 'payeeUserId' for authorization independence.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the payment transaction.

definition.description: Records user ratings for rides/services. Accessible by the rater, rated user, and Admins/SuperAdmins. Includes denormalized 'raterUserId' and 'ratedUserId' for authorization independence.

params.name: ratingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the rating entry.

definition.description: Defines geographic areas for geofencing, service regions, etc. Managed and accessible only by Admins/SuperAdmins.

params.name: zoneId
params.description: The unique identifier for the geographic zone.

definition.description: Records important system actions for security and debugging. Written by Firebase Functions or server-side logic, and readable only by Admins/SuperAdmins. Includes 'userId' of the actor for traceability.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for the audit log entry.

definition.description: Defines configurable vehicle types, categories, capacities, and associated fare multipliers for the platform. Managed and accessible only by Admins/SuperAdmins. Serves as a lookup for vehicle-related business logic.

params.name: typeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a platform-defined vehicle type.

definition.description: A single document for global platform configurations (e.g., API keys, branding, surge pricing algorithms, default settings). Managed exclusively by SuperAdmins.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy for achieving **Authorization Independence** is extensive denormalization, copying relevant user IDs into subcollection documents and related top-level documents. This eliminates the need for `get()` operations in security rules, which simplifies rule logic and prevents cascading authorization failures in atomic operations. For example, `ride` documents denormalize `passengerId` and `driverId`, allowing simple, direct authorization checks for both parties.

**Structural Segregation** is applied by ensuring each collection maintains a homogeneous security posture. For instance, driver-specific documents like `DriverDocument` and `FuelLog` are nested under `/drivers/{driverId}`, ensuring only the relevant driver (or an Admin/SuperAdmin) can access them. Global entities like `zones` and `platform_vehicle_types` are managed centrally by administrative roles.

**Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs)** are enabled by aligning authorization with data ownership and explicit IDs. For drivers, querying their `fuel_logs` or `documents` becomes a simple path-based lookup (`/drivers/{request.auth.uid}/fuel_logs`). For passengers, listing their `rides` involves a `where('passengerId', '==', request.auth.uid)` query, which is efficient and secure. Real-time features are supported by dedicated `live_driver_locations/{driverId}` documents, which denormalize `currentPassengerId` to allow passengers to track their assigned driver's location without needing a `get()` call to the `ride` document. Historical ride locations are nested under `/rides/{rideId}/locations/{locationId}`, also denormalizing relevant user IDs from the parent ride document.

Role-Based Access Control (DBAC) is implemented by storing the `role` in the `/users/{userId}` document. Admins and SuperAdmins are granted broader access based on this role, while individual users (drivers, passengers) only access their own data or data explicitly shared with them via denormalized IDs. The use of descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{driverId}`, `{vehicleId}`) and consistent naming conventions further enhances clarity and debuggability of security rules.
